title: canarium odontophyllum
intro: Canarium odontophyllum, also known as the "Kadupul tree" or "Queen of the Night," is a tropical plant species native to Sri Lanka and other parts of Asia, characterized by its fragrant flowers that bloom at night and wilt before dawn. The plant's bark, seeds, and leaves are used in traditional medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Canarium odontophyllum are believed to provide various health benefits, including antioxidant, antiseptic, and anti-allergic effects, making it a valuable herb in natural remedies. In herbal preparations, the plant's extracts are often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams, showcasing its versatility in traditional medicine and modern applications.
